findings.title: Access-log formatting can read past caller-provided C key buffers
findings.impact: When a C API call supplies key buffers with explicit lengths but a missing length array on the new lazy access-log path, the operation path skips consuming the nested keys, but the recorder still reaches objectKeysToString and falls back to strlen(cKey[i]). A non-NUL-terminated caller buffer can make logging scan past the declared key memory and persist adjacent process memory, including sensitive data, into access logs.
findings.fix: Do not fall back to strlen for APIs whose buffers are length-delimited. Require cKeyLens for this overload, skip logging when it is null, or pass an empty bounded representation; also guard OCPut so NestedKeysRef is called only when both cNestedObjectKeys and cNestedObjKeyLenArray are present.
